title: Concern for jailed Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi as family say health deteriorating
description: On Friday her family said the 54-year-old had been taken from prison to a local hospital after a sharp deterioration in her health.
content:
Relatives of jailed Iranian human rights campaigner Narges Mohammadi and the Nobel Peace Prize Committee have called for her to be transferred to adequate medical facilities following a sharp reported deterioration of her health.

title: Brixton drive-by shooting ‘act of indiscriminate violence’, say Met police
description: Four people are in hospital, with one 25-year-old man facing life-threatening injuries
content:
A drive-by shooting in Brixton which left four people in hospital on Saturday has been called “an act of indiscriminate violence” by police.
Shots were fired in the early hours on Coldharbour Lane in the south London area, leaving one 25-year-old man... [1392 chars]

title: Gyökeres double fires Arsenal past Fulham to turn title pressure on Manchester City
description: Viktor Gyökeres scored twice either side of a Bukayo Saka goal as Arsenal beat Fulham 3-0 to extend their lead at the top of the Premier League to six points
content: Arsenal supporters had almost forgotten what it was like to win without their nerves being shredded. They were reacquainted with the feeling on an occasion when everything felt right from the start and got better and better. It was all over by half-t... [5220 chars]

title: King launches Space Agency project on final day in Bermuda
description: The King concludes his visit to the British overseas territory after landing from the US on Thursday.
content:
King Charles has visited a new UK Space Agency observatory and launched a project to track space debris on the final day of his royal visit to Bermuda.
After opening a new coast guard station later in the day, the King donned his sunglasses as he cli... [2334 chars]

title: Ipswich Town: Are Tractor Boys better equipped for Premier League?
description: Ipswich Town have won promotion from the Championship for the second time in three years - but are they better equipped for the Premier League in 2026?
content:
It's been a great season for Ipswich Town fans, who saw their team do the double over Norwich City in the East Anglian derbies and, oh yes, also win promotion back to the Premier League.
